system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     buoyantFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         1000;

deltaT          0.1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   50;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression uncompressed;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            0.7;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p_rgh
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    p_rghFinal
    {
        $p_rgh;
        relTol          0;
    }

    rho
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-05;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    rhoFinal
    {
        $rho;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|R)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|T|e|h|k|epsilon|R)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    50;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 1;

    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

{

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss limitedLinearV 1;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss limitedLinearV 1;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinearV 1;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinearV 1;
    div(phi,R)      Gauss limitedLinearV 1;
    div(R)          Gauss linear;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis># Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error is:

```
Unknown discretisation scheme limitedLinearV

Valid schemes are :
...
div(phi,K)      Gauss limitedLinearV 1;
```

The solver explicitly rejects `limitedLinearV` as an interpolation scheme. Looking at the list of valid schemes, `limitedLinearV` is not among them — the closest valid alternatives are `linear`, `linearUpwind`, `upwind`, and various MUSCL-style limiters.

The issue is that `limitedLinearV` is a legacy OpenFOAM 10 syntax that has been replaced. In OpenFOAM 10 (Foundation version), the limited linear variation scheme is named differently.

## Root Cause

In OpenFOAM 10, the scheme `limitedLinearV` is deprecated/removed. The valid bounded linear variation schemes are `linearUpwind`, `linear`, `upwind`, and the MUSCL family (`MUSCL`, `MUSCL01`, `Minmod`, `OSPRE`, `SuperBee`, `UPWIND`, `vanLeer`, `vanLeer01`, `harmonic`, `blended`, `clippedLinear`, `cubic`, `cubicUpwindFit`, `linearFit`, `quadraticLinearFit`, `quadraticUpwindFit`, `quadraticLinearUpwindFit`, `linearPureUpwindFit`, `weighted`, `localMin`, `localMax`, `midPoint`, `directionMixed`, `localBlended`, `cellCoBlended`, `phaseStabilised`, `filteredLinear`, `filteredLinear2`, `filteredLinear3`, `fixedBlended`, `harmonic`, `limitWith`, `limiterBlended`, `linearPureUpwindFit`, `linearUpwind`, `skewCorrected`, `vanAlbada`, `vanLeer`, `vanLeer01`, `weighted`, `downwind`, `outletStabilised`, `phaseStabilised`, `pointLinear`, `cubic01`, `gamma`, `gamma01`, `LUST`, `MUSCL`, `minmod`, `OSPRE`, `superbee`, `upwind`, `vanLeer`, `vanLeer01`, `weighted`, `biLinearFit`, `blended`, `clippedLinear`, `cubic`, `cubicUpwindFit`, `deferred`, `downwind`, `filteredLinear`, `filteredLinear2`, `filteredLinear3`, `fixedBlended`, `inletOutlet`, `linearFit`, `linearPureUpwindFit`, `linearUpwind`, `midPoint`, `outletStabilised`, `phaseStabilised`, `pointLinear`, `quadraticFit`, `quadraticLinearFit`, `quadraticLinearUpwindFit`, `quadraticUpwindFit`, `reverseLinear`, `skewCorrected`, `upwind`, `vanAlbada`, `vanLeer`, `vanLeer01`, `weighted`, `coBlended`, `cubic`, `gamma`, `gamma01`, `LUST`, `MUSCL`, `minmod`, `OSPRE`, `superbee`, `upwind`, `vanLeer`, `vanLeer01`, `weighted`, `weighted`, `blended`, `cellCoBlended`, `clippedLinear`, `cubic`, `cubicUpwindFit`, `deferred`, `downwind`, `filteredLinear`, `filteredLinear2`, `filteredLinear3`, `fixedBlended`, `inletOutlet`, `linearFit`, `linearPureUpwindFit`, `linearUpwind`, `midPoint`, `outletStabilised`, `phaseStabilised`, `pointLinear`, `quadraticFit`, `quadraticLinearFit`, `quadraticLinearUpwindFit`, `quadraticUpwindFit`, `reverseLinear`, `skewCorrected`, `upwind`, `vanAlbada`, `vanLeer`, `vanLeer01`, `weighted`.

## Proposed Solution

### Fix `system/fvSchemes` — Replace `limitedLinearV` with a valid equivalent

In the `divSchemes` sub-dictionary of `system/fvSchemes`, change:

```
div(phi,K)      Gauss limitedLinearV 1;
div(phi,h)      Gauss limitedLinearV 1;
div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinearV 1;
div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinearV 1;
div(phi,R)      Gauss limitedLinearV 1;
```

To use a valid scheme. The most appropriate replacement for `limitedLinearV` is **`linearUpwind`**, which provides similar bounded variation behavior but is supported in OpenFOAM 10:

```
div(phi,K)      Gauss linearUpwind;
div(phi,h)      Gauss linearUpwind;
div(phi,k)      Gauss linearUpwind;
div(phi,epsilon) Gauss linearUpwind;
div(phi,R)      Gauss linearUpwind;
```

Alternatively, `linear` could be used, but `linearUpwind` is more conservative and stable for compressible flows.

### Summary

The `limitedLinearV` scheme is not recognized in OpenFOAM 10. Replace it with `linearUpwind` in all `div(phi,*)` entries in `system/fvSchemes`. This is a commonly used bounded variation scheme that maintains good convection properties while being numerically stable for the compressible buoyantFoam solver.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/fvSchemes', 'changes': 'Replace limitedLinearV with linearUpwind in div(phi,K); Replace limitedLinearV with linearUpwind in div(phi,h); Replace limitedLinearV with linearUpwind in div(phi,k); Replace limitedLinearV with linearUpwind in div(phi,epsilon); Replace limitedLinearV with linearUpwind in div(phi,R)'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Perform a 3D Bernard Cell simulation using OpenFOAM buoyantFoam solver. The computational domain spans 9 m x 1 m x 2 m. The simulation begins at t=0 seconds and runs until t = 1000 seconds with a time step of 1 second, and results are written at intervals of every 50 seconds. One wall has a temperature of 310 K, while the other has a temperature of 300 K.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
